seed_problem: Build a connected relay network where no node has degree > 3, minimizing total cable length; checker validates connectivity+degree and scores baseline/cost.
mutation: output: keep the min-length connectivity goal but restrict the output to a per-station degree-capped connected spanning network (caps in {2,3,4}), turning the polynomial Euclidean minimum spanning tree into the NP-hard degree-constrained minimum spanning tree.
title: Prospector Relay Grid: Degree-Capped Asteroid Comms
strategies: chain baseline: link the stations in input order into a single path (always feasible, matches the checker baseline, ratio ~0.1)
nearest-neighbour path: start anywhere and repeatedly hop to the closest unvisited station, forming a short degree-2 chain
degree-constrained Kruskal/Prim: grow a spanning tree by adding the globally shortest links whose endpoints still have free relay heads, repairing any leftover components via spare-capacity nodes
local search: start from a degree-constrained tree and apply length-reducing edge swaps / branch reroutes that respect the caps
